title: Baldrs draumar
stanzas: Once Balder dreamed a dream of dread,
A nightmare filled with shadows dark,
He woke in fear and spoke thereof,
To Odin All-Father in his hall.
Then Odin rode to Hel's abode,
To wake the Völva from her sleep,
To ask her what the dream did mean,
And what the future had in store.
The Völva spoke from out the earth,
'A child shall come with sword of flame,
And Balder's fate shall soon be sealed,
By Loki's cunning and the mistletoe's might.'
Odin asked, 'What means this doom?'
'What force shall be so great and fell,
'As to bring about the end of Balder,
'So fair and good and bright of soul?'
'The blind god shall throw the mistletoe,
'At Balder's breast with Loki's aid,
'And he shall fall in death's dark sleep,
'And mourn him shall all that is good and fair.'
Then Odin rode back to Asgard,
'Resolved to avert the doom foretold,
'He journeyed to every land and place,
'To make all things swear not to harm his son.'
From every stone and every tree,
From metal bright and soil and flame,
From all the creatures, great and small,
'The oath they took not to do him wrong.'
But of the mistletoe, Odin asked not,
'Thinking it too young and weak,
'To ever cause harm to Balder fair,
'And so the doom was sealed by fate.'
Then Loki came with cunning craft,
'And made the mistletoe sharp and hard,
'And put it in the blind god's hand,
'And guided him to Balder there.'
The shaft flew forth and struck him down,
'And Balder fell as the Völva said,
'And all of Asgard wept in grief,
'For Balder was the light of all.'
